commands.review_summary:
The branch removes non-direct suppression for classified rate-limit and overload auto-reply failures, marks non-silent failure payloads as errors, and broadens runner tests across representative non-direct surfaces.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +19. Total +20 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level, but not as live behavior: current main passes suppressInNonDirect for rate-limit/overload failures, and the PR tests show that path becomes visible after the patch. A real transport reproduction is still missing.

commands.author: fuller-stack-dev

target.title: fix(auto-reply): surface fatal channel errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Surface classified rate-limit and overload runner failures in non-direct channel contexts instead of routing them to `NO_REPLY`.
- Keep generic runner failure boilerplate silent in non-direct chats unless the existing silent-reply policy explicitly disallows silence.
- Mark non-silent agent-run failure payloads as `isError` so downstream delivery sees them as error notices.

## Upstream/channel coverage

This change is in the shared auto-reply runner before plugin-specific delivery. The visibility decision is made in `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation()` from the shared `TemplateContext.ChatType` plus silent-reply policy, not from Discord-specific delivery code.

The regression tests now exercise representative non-direct surfaces through the same core path:

- Discord group
- Discord channel
- Slack channel
- Telegram group
- WhatsApp group
- Microsoft Teams channel

For those surfaces, tests assert that generic raw runner failures still resolve to `NO_REPLY` under default silent policy, while classified rate-limit and overload failures produce visible `isError` payloads.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
  - Result: passed, 177 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - Result: clean.
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
  - Result: passed.
  - Provider: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.
  - Testbox: `tbx_01kt62mj2zsghcn3anmfk57aym`.
  - Actions run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26867691876.
  - Summary: `command=2m20.945s total=2m29.427s exit=0`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: fatal channel-visible rate-limit/overload failures are no longer suppressed to `NO_REPLY` in non-direct channel contexts, while generic runner failures remain silent by default.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox using the OpenClaw changed-check lane, plus focused local Vitest through the repo node wrapper.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: regression tests assert Discord, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp, and Microsoft Teams non-direct contexts all use the shared core route: classified rate-limit and overloaded failures produce non-`NO_REPLY` `isError` payloads, while generic raw runner failures still resolve to `NO_REPLY` under default silent-reply policy. The changed gate passed on Testbox `tbx_01kt62mj2zsghcn3anmfk57aym`.

Observed result after fix: classified transient failure copy is visible for representative non-direct surfaces; generic raw runner failure copy still resolves to `NO_REPLY` under default silent-reply policy.

What was not tested: a live Discord roundtrip with a real provider outage was not run; the behavior was validated at the shared auto-reply runner layer before channel-specific transport delivery.
